# Bootstrap Component Blox (BCB)

> A WordPress parent theme built on Bootstrap 5 for building client sites via child themes.

## Overview

BCB provides a lightweight, stable foundation with Bootstrap 5.3.8, Font Awesome 6.7.2, and Bootstrap Icons 1.13.1. All site-specific work happens in child themes — the parent is never modified directly.

## Architecture

- Parent theme defines the framework: grid, nav, widgets, helpers, hooks
- Child themes define the project: colors, typography, components, page templates
- Two manifest files drive all development (see below)
- All templates are orchestrators — they only call `get_template_part()`
- Each page gets its own scoped CSS file, conditionally enqueued

## Manifest System

Three markdown files serve as the single source of truth:

1. `bcb-manifest-core.md` (parent theme) — compact quick reference: helper function signatures, file structure, section pattern, scoped CSS rules, responsive rules, enqueue pattern, safety constraints. Always read first.
2. `bcb-manifest.md` (parent theme) — full API reference: complete code examples, CPT patterns, CSS architecture, Swiper/GSAP boilerplate, hooks, customizer settings. Read when building sections or pages.
3. `project-manifest.md` (child theme) — project config: CSS prefix, color palette, typography, button variants, image registry, page templates, component classes

An AI agent should read `bcb-manifest-core.md` + `project-manifest.md` before any work, and load `bcb-manifest.md` when building new sections or pages.

## Key Helper Functions

All prefixed with `bcb_`:

- `bcb_image_url($id, $size)` — returns attachment URL by ID
- `bcb_image_alt($id)` — returns attachment alt text by ID
- `bcb_icon($name, $size)` — renders Bootstrap Icon via CDN font
- `bcb_main_nav($classes)` — outputs main navigation
- `bcb_aux_nav($classes)` — outputs auxiliary navigation
- `bcb_check_template_name($name)` — checks current page template filename
- `bcb_get_the_content($id)` — renders any post's content by ID
- `bcb_pagination()` — outputs Bootstrap-styled pagination
- `bcb_taxonomy_terms($tax, $fields, $separator)` — returns taxonomy term string

## Action Hooks for Extension

- `bcb_before_navbar` / `bcb_after_navbar` — inject content around navbar
- `bcb_before_footer` — inject content before footer
- `bcb_customizer_panels` / `bcb_customizer_sections` / `bcb_customizer_settings` / `bcb_customizer_controls` — extend the Customizer

## Child Theme File Structure

```
child-theme/
├── style.css                        CSS variables, utilities, shared classes
├── functions.php                    Enqueue, setup, conditional loads
├── header-scripts.php               Google Fonts and external <link> tags
├── acf-json/                        ACF local JSON sync (auto-generated)
├── css/{context}.css                Scoped stylesheets (one per page/view)
├── js/                              Custom JS (animations, interactions)
├── templates/{page}.php             Page template orchestrators
├── archive.php                      Default archive (category, tag, date, author)
├── archive-{cpt}.php               CPT archive orchestrators
├── single-{cpt}.php                CPT single orchestrators
└── template-parts/
    ├── common/                      Shared parts reused across pages
    └── {context}/
        └── {context}-{section}.php  Section markup
```

## Section Template Pattern

Every section follows:
```php
<section id="{prefix}-{name}" class="px-3 py-4 p-lg-5">
    <div class="container">
        <div class="row">
            <!-- content -->
        </div>
    </div>
</section>
```

## CSS Conventions

- Every project uses a unique prefix (e.g. `gec-`, `tc-`)
- CSS variables: `--{prefix}-primary`, `--{prefix}-dark`, `--{prefix}-font`, `--{prefix}-font-header`
- Button variants: `.{prefix}-btn-primary`, `.{prefix}-btn-dark`, `.{prefix}-btn-light`, `.{prefix}-btn-orange`
- Scoped CSS per page — one file per view in `css/`
- `style.css` is read-only for agents unless explicitly permitted

## Conditional Enqueue Pattern

```php
if (bcb_check_template_name('about.php')) {
    wp_enqueue_style('about', get_stylesheet_directory_uri() . '/css/about.css');
}
```

## For Full Reference

Read `bcb-manifest.md` in the parent theme directory for complete documentation.
